You are surveying candidate "audit pilot pairs" in the repo at /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster. A pilot pair = one AsciiDoc spec subdocument plus its implementing bash module(s), which will be audited together under the Allocation Coding Guide (ACG). The audit looks for: (a) "conceptual comments" in bash source — comments carrying design-time knowledge (what a thing IS, why it's shaped so, how it relates to other concepts) rather than operational mechanics; (b) "ornament" in the spec — sentences in normative position that neither constrain an implementer nor are marked as rationale; (c) paraphrase — the same normative idea worded differently in spec vs source comments.

Candidate area: the CRUCIBLE RUNTIME — sentry and bottle.
- Specs: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSSR-sentry_run.adoc, RBSSS-sentry_start.adoc, RBSBL-bottle_launch.adoc, RBSBC-bottle_create.adoc, RBSBK-bottle_cleanup.adoc, and glance at RBSII-iptables_init.adoc, RBSNC-network_create.adoc
- Sources: Tools/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh, Tools/rbk/rbob_bottle.sh

Report, with search breadth medium:
1. Pair coherence: which spec(s) map onto rbjs_sentry.sh and which onto rbob_bottle.sh? Propose the cleanest one-spec-or-spec-cluster ↔ one-module pairing for each.
2. Audit richness: quote 3-5 short specimens of design-time/conceptual comments in each source file (file:line + comment text, truncated) — security rationale comments (why iptables rules exist, what the enclave model is) are prime specimens. Quote 2-3 specimens of spec prose that looks like unmarked ornament or restates something the source also states.
3. Sizes: line counts of the specs and the two source files.
4. Churn: run `git log --oneline --since="2026-05-01" -- <the files>` and report commit counts, and whether messages carry heat tags ₣BH or ₣BZ.
5. Verdict line per pair (sentry, bottle): rich and quiet, or thin, or hot/contested?

Return a compact structured report. Do not modify anything.

- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	//axhob_operation
2	{mkr_sentry_run}
3	
4	NOTE: Sentry container creation and startup is managed by Docker Compose.
5	The compose service definition specifies the sentry image, privileged mode, dual network attachment (default + enclave with static IP), environment variables forwarded from the nameplate, and the health check.
6	
7	The sentry's `CMD` is its baked-in entrypoint script (`/opt/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh`), COPYed into the image during the vessel build.
8	No volume mounts are used for security container scripts — the baked entrypoint is the only code that runs.
9	
10	//axhoc_completion
11	After compose starts the sentry, the baked entrypoint executes: discovers interfaces by IP, configures iptables, starts dnsmasq, and signals health.
12	The compose health check (`test -f /tmp/rbjh_healthy`) gates downstream containers.
13

1	//axhob_operation
2	{opss_sentry_start}
3	
4	NOTE: Docker Compose orchestrates the full container lifecycle declaratively.
5	The sentry starts first; its health check (`/tmp/rbjh_healthy`) gates the pentacle; the pentacle's health check (`/tmp/rbjph_healthy`) gates the bottle.
6	Network creation, container instantiation, and startup ordering are all managed by compose — the sub-operations below describe what happens inside each container at startup, not imperative orchestration steps.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. *Compose creates networks and starts sentry*:
10	.. Compose creates
11	{at_transit_network}
12	(`transit`) and
13	{at_enclave_network}
14	(`enclave` with static subnet from nameplate)
15	.. Sentry container starts on both networks with privileged mode
16	.. Baked-in entrypoint (`rbjs_sentry.sh`) executes
17	
18	//axhos_step
19	. *Sentry discovers interfaces and takes ownership of routing*:
20	.. Sentry uses `ip -o addr show to «enclave_ip»` to identify which interface carries the enclave IP
21	.. The other global-scope interface is identified as the uplink
22	.. All iptables rules use discovered interface names, not hardcoded eth0/eth1
23	.. NOTE: Docker Compose does not guarantee network-to-interface ordering; IP-based discovery is required
24	.. Sentry replaces the default route with one via the uplink gateway, computed as the first usable IP of the uplink subnet (Docker bridge convention)
25	.. NOTE: Docker assigns the default route to the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway (empirically, on both linux engine 28.x and mac Docker Desktop 28.x); compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` does NOT influence this. Sentry-side ownership is the load-bearing fix — same don't-trust-Docker discipline that motivates IP-based interface discovery.
26	.. NOTE: Sentry takes ownership of any runtime-implicit behavior its security envelope depends on.
27	   This applies to interface naming (IP-based discovery, above), default-route selection (explicit override post-discovery, above), published-port target selection (iptables rules that do not depend on which interface the framework chose for delivery), and any future runtime-implicit ordering that emerges.
28	   Kernel state and sentry's own iptables are the source of truth; framework-implicit ordering is not load-bearing for the security envelope.
29	
30	//axhos_step
31	. *Sentry configures iptables*:
32	.. Default policies: DROP on INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT
33	.. Custom chains: RBM-INGRESS, RBM-EGRESS, RBM-FORWARD
34	.. ICMP allowed within enclave only; ICMP cross-boundary blocked
35	.. Entry-port DNAT plus POSTROUTING MASQUERADE and conntrack-DNAT-state FORWARD authorization if {rbrn_entry_mode} is enabled — see {scr_port_setup} for the iptables rules and kernel sysctls (rp_filter loose, ip_local_port_range) the entry-port path requires
36	.. Access and DNS rules per nameplate configuration (allowlist/global/disabled modes)
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Sentry starts dnsmasq*:
40	.. Listens on enclave interface at
41	{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
42	.. Domain filtering per
43	{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
44	(allowlist or global)
45	.. DNS firewall rules added to RBM-INGRESS and RBM-EGRESS
46	
47	//axhos_step
48	. *Sentry signals health*:
49	.. Creates `/tmp/rbjh_healthy`
50	.. Compose health check detects this and allows pentacle to start
51	
52	//axhoc_completion
53	After sentry is healthy, the
54	{at_sentry_container}
55	has dual network connectivity with iptables enforcement and DNS services active, ready to serve the
56	{rbsc_crucible}.
57

1	//axhob_operation
2	{mkr_bottle_launch}
3	
4	NOTE: Bottle container creation is managed by Docker Compose.
5	The bottle service definition specifies the bottle image, `network_mode: "service:pentacle"` (shares pentacle's network namespace), `cap_drop: NET_RAW, NET_ADMIN` and `security_opt: label:disable` (kernel-layer capability bounds), and a `sessile` profile gate.
6	Volume mounts are declared in the per-nameplate compose fragment (`«moniker».compose.yml`), not in nameplate env variables.
7	
8	//axhos_step
9	. *Compose starts bottle after pentacle health check*:
10	.. The bottle's `depends_on` with `condition: service_healthy` ensures the pentacle has completed routing setup before the bottle starts
11	.. The bottle inherits the pentacle's network namespace — it sees the enclave interface with routing through the sentry already configured
12	.. No network configuration occurs inside the bottle container
13	
14	//axhos_step
15	. *Capability and label bounds*:
16	.. `cap_drop: NET_RAW, NET_ADMIN` removes the bottle process's ability to create raw sockets or modify network configuration from inside its namespace.
17	   These bounds are kernel-layer defense-in-depth peer to the iptables-layer enforcement at sentry.
18	.. `security_opt: label:disable` disables SELinux/AppArmor labelling for the bottle, required for the volume bind-mount workflow used by per-nameplate compose fragments.
19	   The capability bounds above are the load-bearing kernel-layer controls; label:disable does not weaken them.
20	
21	//axhoc_completion
22	The bottle starts with a pre-configured network path through the sentry.
23	Security enforcement is active from the first packet.
24

3	
4	NOTE: This sequence within
5	{rbsc_enjoin}

7	{at_bottle_container}

9	Compose inherits the full bottle service definition — image, network mode, volumes, and dependencies — from the base compose file and optional nameplate fragment.

12	. *Compose resolves container configuration*:
13	.. Image: `{rbrn_bottle_vessel}:{rbrn_bottle_hallmark}` from compose service definition
14	.. Network: inherits `network_mode: "service:pentacle"` (shares pentacle's network namespace, which routes through sentry)
15	.. Volumes: declared in per-nameplate compose fragment (`«moniker».compose.yml`), not in nameplate env variables
16	.. Environment: forwarded from compose env-files and service `environment:` section
17	
18	//axhos_step
19	. *Compose creates transient container*:
20	.. Container created from resolved service definition with `--rm` (automatic removal on exit)
21	.. Compose assigns container name (not user-specified for transient runs)
22	.. {rbbc_require}
23	container created successfully
24	
25	//axhoc_completion
26	Successful creation yields a transient container with the same security posture as a sessile bottle — same network namespace, same volume mounts, same sentry enforcement.
27

1	//axhob_operation
2	{mkr_bottle_cleanup}
3	
4	NOTE: Cleanup of prior state is handled by `docker compose down --remove-orphans` within
5	{rbsc_charge}.
6	This removes all containers and networks for the nameplate's compose project in a single command.
7	Tolerates missing projects (first start on a fresh system).
8	
9	After compose-down, a subnet conflict detection step checks whether any foreign Docker network occupies this nameplate's enclave subnet.
10	If a stale network from a different compose project is found (e.g., containers died without compose-down after a reboot), the operation fails with an actionable cleanup command.

4	Establishes foundational security state:
5	
6	//axhos_step
7	. Base Chain Configuration:
8	* Flush all rules from filter and nat tables
9	* Set policy DROP on all base chains (INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT)
10	* Accept loopback interface traffic unconditionally as the first rule in the INPUT chain and as the first rule in the OUTPUT chain
11	
12	//axhos_step
13	. State Tracking Setup:
14	* Insert RELATED,ESTABLISHED tracking as second rule in chains:
15	** INPUT chain with "-m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT"
16	** FORWARD chain with same state match
17	** OUTPUT chain with same state match
18	
19	//axhos_step
20	. RBM Chain Creation:
21	* Create in filter table with default policy DROP:
22	** RBM-INGRESS for input control
23	** RBM-EGRESS for output control
24	** RBM-FORWARD for forwarding control
25	* Insert jumps as third rule in respective base chains:
26	** INPUT jumps to RBM-INGRESS
27	** OUTPUT jumps to RBM-EGRESS
28	** FORWARD jumps to RBM-FORWARD
29	
30	//axhos_step
31	. ICMP Within Enclave:
32	* Accept ICMP arriving on the interface attached to {at_enclave_network} in RBM-INGRESS
33	* Accept ICMP departing on the interface attached to {at_enclave_network} in RBM-EGRESS

35	//axhos_step
36	. ICMP Cross-Boundary Block:
37	* Drop all ICMP in RBM-FORWARD
38	* Drop ICMP departing on the interface attached to {at_transit_network} in RBM-EGRESS

1	#!/bin/sh
2	echo "RBJ: Beginning sentry setup script"
3	
4	set -e
5	test "${RBJ_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 1 && set -x
6	
7	echo "RBJp0: Validate compose env-file quoting"
8	: "${RBJE_PROBE:?}"
9	z_probe_count=0
10	for z_word in ${RBJE_PROBE}; do
11	  z_probe_count=$((z_probe_count + 1))
12	done
13	test "${z_probe_count}" -eq 2                || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE token count ${z_probe_count}, expected 2 — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
14	test "$(echo ${RBJE_PROBE} | cut -d' ' -f1)" = "alpha" || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE first token not 'alpha' — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
15	test "$(echo ${RBJE_PROBE} | cut -d' ' -f2)" = "bravo" || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE second token not 'bravo' — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
16	echo "RBJp0: Compose env-file quoting validated — RBJE_PROBE = '${RBJE_PROBE}'"
17	
18	echo "RBJp1: Validate parameters"
19	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP        = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}"
20	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK        = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
21	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP:?}"      && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP      = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"
22	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP:?}"      && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP      = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
23	: "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER:?}"             && echo "RBJp0: RBRR_DNS_SERVER             = ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"
24	: "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE:?}"             && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_MODE             = ${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}"
25	: "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:?}" && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION = ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
26	: "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:?}"     && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE     = ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}"
27	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE        = ${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}"
28	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN        = ${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}"
29	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE:?}"     && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE     = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}"
30	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS:?}"   && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS   = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}"
31	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS:?}" && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}"
32	
33	echo "RBJp1: Discovering network interfaces by IP (Docker does not guarantee eth0/eth1 ordering)"
34	z_temp_file="/tmp/rbj_iface_discovery.txt"
35	
36	ip -o addr show to "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" > "${z_temp_file}" || exit 11
37	read z_num RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF z_rest < "${z_temp_file}"
38	test -n "${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}" || { echo "FATAL: No interface found with IP ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"; exit 11; }
39	
40	ip -o -4 addr show scope global > "${z_temp_file}" || exit 11
41	RBJ_UPLINK_IF=""
42	while read z_num z_ifname z_rest; do
43	  test "${z_ifname}" = "${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}" && continue
44	  RBJ_UPLINK_IF="${z_ifname}"
45	  break
46	done < "${z_temp_file}"
47	test -n "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" || { echo "FATAL: No uplink interface found"; exit 11; }
48	
49	rm -f "${z_temp_file}"
50	echo "RBJp1: Enclave interface = ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"
51	echo "RBJp1: Uplink interface  = ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"
52

54	# gateway it picks (alphabetically-first attached, empirically); compose 'priority'
55	# is not honored. Same don't-trust-Docker discipline as interface naming above —
56	# sentry takes ownership: derive uplink gateway as first usable IP of uplink

58	echo "RBJp1: Computing uplink gateway from connected subnet"
59	z_uplink_addr_cidr=$(ip -o -4 addr show dev "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" | awk '{print $4; exit}')
60	test -n "${z_uplink_addr_cidr}" || { echo "FATAL: No IPv4 address on uplink interface ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"; exit 12; }
61	z_uplink_ip="${z_uplink_addr_cidr%/*}"
62	z_uplink_prefix="${z_uplink_addr_cidr#*/}"
63	
64	IFS=. read z_o1 z_o2 z_o3 z_o4 <<EOF_OCTETS
65	${z_uplink_ip}
66	EOF_OCTETS
67	
68	z_host_bits=$(( 32 - z_uplink_prefix ))
69	z_ip_int=$(( (z_o1 << 24) | (z_o2 << 16) | (z_o3 << 8) | z_o4 ))
70	z_mask=$(( 0xFFFFFFFF ^ ((1 << z_host_bits) - 1) ))
71	z_net_int=$(( z_ip_int & z_mask ))
72	z_gw_int=$(( z_net_int + 1 ))
73	RBJ_UPLINK_GW="$(( (z_gw_int >> 24) & 0xFF )).$(( (z_gw_int >> 16) & 0xFF )).$(( (z_gw_int >> 8) & 0xFF )).$(( z_gw_int & 0xFF ))"
74	echo "RBJp1: Uplink gateway     = ${RBJ_UPLINK_GW}"
75	
76	echo "RBJp1: Replacing default route via uplink (sentry-side ownership)"
77	ip route replace default via "${RBJ_UPLINK_GW}" dev "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" || exit 13
78	ip -o -4 route show default
79	
80	echo "RBJp1: Beginning IPTables initialization"
81	
82	echo "RBJp1: Set ephemeral port range for uplink connections"
83	echo "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN} 65535" > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_local_port_range || exit 10
84	
85	echo "RBJp1: Flushing existing rules"
86	iptables -F        || exit 10
87	iptables -t nat -F || exit 10
88	
89	echo "RBJp1: Setting default policies"
90	iptables -P INPUT   DROP || exit 10
91	iptables -P FORWARD DROP || exit 10
92	iptables -P OUTPUT  DROP || exit 10
93	
94	echo "RBJp1: Configuring loopback access"
95	iptables -A INPUT  -i lo -j ACCEPT || exit 10
96	iptables -A OUTPUT -o lo -j ACCEPT || exit 10
97	
98	echo "RBJp1: Setting up connection tracking"
99	iptables -A INPUT   -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10
100	iptables -A FORWARD -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10
101	iptables -A OUTPUT  -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10
102	
103	echo "RBJp1: Creating RBM chains"
104	iptables -N RBM-INGRESS || exit 10
105	iptables -N RBM-EGRESS  || exit 10
106	iptables -N RBM-FORWARD || exit 10
107	
108	echo "RBJp1: Setting up chain jumps"
109	iptables -A INPUT   -j RBM-INGRESS || exit 10
110	iptables -A OUTPUT  -j RBM-EGRESS  || exit 10
111	iptables -A FORWARD -j RBM-FORWARD || exit 10
112	
113	echo "RBJp2: Allowing ICMP within enclave only"
114	iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p icmp -j ACCEPT || exit 20
115	iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p icmp -j ACCEPT || exit 20
116	
117	echo "RBJp2: Enabling IP forwarding (required whenever sentry forwards on behalf of the enclave)"
118	echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward || exit 25
119	
120	if test "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" = "rbnne_enabled"; then
121	  echo "RBJp2c: Relaxing rp_filter to loose mode (required for interface-agnostic ingress)"
122	  # rp_filter=1 (strict) requires the packet's source-IP reverse-path to match the
123	  # ingress interface. Inbound published-port traffic from the framework gateway
124	  # (e.g., Docker Desktop 192.168.65.1) arrives on whichever interface Docker chose
125	  # for port-publish delivery, but its reverse route is always via the default
126	  # gateway on uplink — strict mode mismatches and drops at routing time, before
127	  # the iptables FORWARD chain ever sees the packet. Loose mode (rp_filter=2)
128	  # allows any source IP that's reachable via any interface, which is the actual
129	  # invariant for entry-port traffic. Iptables rules below still gate authorization;
130	  # rp_filter is not the load-bearing security control.
131	  echo 2 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter || exit 25
132	
133	  echo "RBJp2c: Configuring entry-port DNAT + MASQUERADE + FORWARD-ACCEPT"
134	  # Classification: destination port + per-IP source exclusion of the two enclave-internal
135	  # containers (sentry and bottle/pentacle). RETURN short-circuit pattern — if source matches
136	  # either enclave container IP, skip the DNAT; otherwise the unconditional DNAT below fires.
137	  #
138	  # Why per-IP and not whole-CIDR (! -s ENCLAVE_CIDR): on linux Docker Engine the host
139	  # attaches to the enclave bridge as a peer with the bridge gateway IP (.1), which is inside
140	  # the enclave CIDR by Docker convention. Whole-CIDR exclusion rejects every legitimate host
141	  # SYN (empirically confirmed on cerebro: PREROUTING DNAT fired 0 times). Per-IP excludes
142	  # exactly the threat surface — the two enclave container IPs known to compose — and lets
143	  # the bridge gateway, Desktop's VM gateway (192.168.65.1), rootlesskit's synthetic source
144	  # (10.0.2.100), and external clients all pass.
145	  #
146	  # Invariant defended: "enclave-internal sources MUST NOT reach sentry's entry port via DNAT"
147	  # (ifrit's direct_sentry_probe and net_dnat_entry_reflection sorties).
148	  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
149	           -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j RETURN || exit 25
150	  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
151	           -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" -j RETURN || exit 25
152	  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
153	           -j DNAT --to-destination "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" || exit 25
154	
155	  # Return-path symmetry: MASQUERADE rewrites the source of the post-DNAT packet to
156	  # sentry's enclave IP, so the bottle's reply destination is in-enclave. The bottle then
157	  # routes via its directly-connected enclave route back to sentry, where conntrack reverses
158	  # the NAT cleanly. Without this, the bottle would reply to the original (possibly
159	  # framework-rewritten) source IP, traversing default-via-sentry into transit — empirically
160	  # broken on Docker Desktop per the topology-reframe scry diagnostic.
161	  iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp \
162	           -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" -j MASQUERADE || exit 25
163	
164	  # Authorization: only flows that sentry's own PREROUTING DNAT created conntrack state
165	  # for are allowed to forward. Conntrack DNAT-state is unforgeable from either bridge.
166	  # The parent FORWARD chain's ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT (line above) handles the return
167	  # path before reaching RBM-FORWARD.
168	  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -p tcp \
169	           -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" \
170	           -m conntrack --ctstate DNAT -j ACCEPT || exit 25
171	fi
172	
173	echo "RBJp2b: Blocking ICMP cross-boundary traffic"
174	iptables -A RBM-FORWARD         -p icmp -j DROP || exit 28
175	iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p icmp -j DROP || exit 28
176	
177	echo "RBJp3: Phase 3: Access Setup"
178	if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}" = "rbnne_disabled"; then
179	  echo "RBJp3: Blocking all non-port traffic"
180	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -j DROP || exit 30
181	  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -j DROP || exit 30
182	else
183	  echo "RBJp3: Setting up uplink network hardening"
184	  echo 0 > /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/all/disable_ipv6    || exit 31
185	  echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}/route_localnet || exit 31
186	
187	  echo "RBJp3: Configuring NAT"
188	  iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}" \
189	                                       ! -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}" \
190	                                       -j MASQUERADE || exit 31
191	
192	  if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}" = "rbnne_global"; then
193	    echo "RBJp3: Enabling global access"
194	    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -j ACCEPT || exit 31
195	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -j ACCEPT || exit 31
196	  else
197	    echo "RBJp3: Configuring DNS server access"
198	    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"        -j ACCEPT || exit 31
199	    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"        -j ACCEPT || exit 31
200	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j ACCEPT || exit 31
201	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j ACCEPT || exit 31
202	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53                                -j DROP   || exit 31
203	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53                                -j DROP   || exit 31
204	
205	    echo "RBJp3: Setting up CIDR-based access control"
206	    for cidr in ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}; do
207	      iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -d "${cidr}" -j ACCEPT || exit 32
208	      iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -d "${cidr}" -j ACCEPT || exit 32
209	    done
210	  fi
211	fi
212	
213	echo "RBJp4: Configuring DNS services"
214	
215	echo "RBJp4: Configuring sentry DNS resolution"
216	echo "nameserver ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" > /etc/resolv.conf   || exit 40
217	
218	if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}" = "rbnne_disabled"; then
219	  echo "RBJp4: Blocking all DNS traffic"
220	  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
221	  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
222	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
223	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
224	else
225	  echo "RBJp4: Set up DNS Server"
226	
227	  echo "RBJp4: Note version in use"
228	  dnsmasq --version
229	
230	  echo "RBJp4: Configuring dnsmasq"
231	  echo "bind-interfaces"                                         > /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
232	  echo "interface=${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"                             >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
233	  echo "listen-address=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"               >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
234	  echo "no-dhcp-interface=${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"                     >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
235	  echo "dns-forward-max=150"                                    >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
236	  echo "cache-size=1000"                                        >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
237	  echo "min-port=4096"                                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
238	  echo "max-port=65535"                                         >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
239	  echo "min-cache-ttl=600"                                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
240	  echo "max-cache-ttl=3600"                                     >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
241	  echo "no-resolv"                                              >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
242	  echo "strict-order"                                           >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
243	  echo "bogus-priv"                                             >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
244	  echo "domain-needed"                                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
245	  echo "except-interface=${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"                       >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
246	  echo "log-queries=extra"                                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
247	  echo "log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log"                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
248	  echo "log-dhcp"                                               >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
249	  echo "log-debug"                                              >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
250	  echo "log-async=20"                                           >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
251	  if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}" = "rbnne_global"; then
252	    echo "RBJp4: Enabling global DNS resolution"
253	    echo "server=${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
254	  else
255	    echo "RBJp4: Resolve-then-freeze — resolve allowed domains via upstream, freeze as static entries"
256	    for domain in ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}; do
257	      echo "RBJp4: Resolving ${domain} via ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"
258	      z_ips=$(dig +short A "${domain}" @"${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^[0-9]+\.' | sort -u)
259	      test -n "${z_ips}" || { echo "FATAL: Cannot resolve ${domain} via ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"; exit 42; }
260	      for z_ip in ${z_ips}; do
261	        echo "RBJp4: Freezing ${domain} -> ${z_ip}"
262	        echo "address=/${domain}/${z_ip}"                    >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
263	      done
264	    done
265	    # No server= lines — no forwarding path exists. Subdomain queries return frozen IPs.
266	    # All non-allowed domains get NXDOMAIN.
267	    echo "address=/#/"                                        >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
268	  fi
269	
270	  echo "RBJp4: Echo back the constructed dnsmasq config file"
271	  cat                                                              /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
272	
273	  echo "RBJp4: Starting dnsmasq service"
274	  dnsmasq
275	  echo "RBJp4: Waiting for dnsmasq to initialize"
276	  sleep 2
277	
278	  echo "RBJp4: Configuring DNS firewall rules"
279	  iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53                         -j ACCEPT || exit 43
280	  iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53                         -j ACCEPT || exit 43
281	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" -j ACCEPT || exit 43
282	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" -j ACCEPT || exit 43
283	fi
284	
285	echo "RBJp5: Signaling health"
286	touch /tmp/rbjh_healthy || exit 50
287	
288	echo "RBJp5: Sentry setup complete, entering hold"
289	exec sleep infinity
290	
291

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBOB - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle
20	# Container lifecycle management via Docker Compose
21	#
22	# Requires: buc_command.sh sourced
23	# Requires: rbrn_regime.sh sourced
24	# Requires: rbrr_regime.sh sourced
25	# Requires: rbrd_regime.sh sourced
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	# Multiple inclusion detection
30	test -z "${ZRBOB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
31	ZRBOB_SOURCED=1
32	
33	
34	######################################################################
35	# Compose-env Validation
36	
37	# Validate that compose-consumed RBRN fields contain no quotes or ${VAR} references.
38	# Compose --env-file does not strip quotes (they become part of the value) and
39	# interpolates ${VAR} references (causing unintended expansion). Fields not consumed
40	# by compose (e.g., RBRN_DESCRIPTION) are exempt.
41	zrbob_validate_compose_env() {
42	  # Explicit list of compose-consumed field names (the nameplate↔compose contract)
43	  local z_compose_fields="
44	    RBRN_MONIKER
45	    RBRN_RUNTIME
46	    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL
47	    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL
48	    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK
49	    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK
50	    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
51	    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
52	    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
53	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
54	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
55	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
56	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
57	    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
58	    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
59	    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
60	    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
61	    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
62	  "
63	
64	  local z_field=""
65	  for z_field in ${z_compose_fields}; do
66	    local z_value="${!z_field:-}"
67	    # Check for quotes (compose does not strip them)
68	    case "${z_value}" in
69	      *\"*|*\'*)
70	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains quotes — compose --env-file does not strip quotes, they become part of the value"
71	        ;;
72	    esac
73	    # Check for ${VAR} references (compose interpolates them)
74	    case "${z_value}" in
75	      *'${'*)
76	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains \${VAR} reference — compose --env-file interpolates these, causing unintended expansion"
77	        ;;
78	    esac
79	  done
80	}
81	
82	######################################################################
83	# Kindle and Sentinel
84	
85	zrbob_kindle() {
86	  test -z "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob already kindled"
87	
88	  # Verify RBRN regime is kindled (provides nameplate config)
89	  zrbrn_sentinel
90	
91	  # Validate compose-consumed fields before proceeding
92	  zrbob_validate_compose_env
93	
94	  # Verify RBRR regime is kindled (provides repo config like DNS_SERVER)
95	  zrbrr_sentinel
96	
97	  # Runtime command (docker or podman)
98	  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
99	    docker) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="docker" ;;
100	    podman) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="podman" ;;
101	    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRN_RUNTIME: ${RBRN_RUNTIME}" ;;
102	  esac
103	
104	  # Compose project identity for this nameplate. The runtime-prefixed moniker
105	  # is the authoritative project name compose labels containers and networks
106	  # under; every -p invocation and every container/network name derives from it.
107	  readonly ZRBOB_PROJECT="${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}"
108	
109	  # Container names (for connect and info commands)
110	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-${RBCC_container_sentry}"
111	  readonly ZRBOB_PENTACLE="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-${RBCC_container_pentacle}"
112	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}-${RBCC_container_bottle}"
113	
114	  # Network name (compose names as {project}_{network}; used by observe and info)
115	  readonly ZRBOB_NETWORK="${ZRBOB_PROJECT}_enclave"
116	
117	  # Base compose file and the compose-quoting probe are kit machinery, not
118	  # consumer config — they live with the kit (RBCC_KIT_DIR), not in moorings.
119	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbob_compose.yml"
120	  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
121	
122	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/rbnnh_compose.yml"
123	  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time
124	
125	  readonly ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/rbnnh_post_charge.sh"
126	  # Hook is optional — existence + executable bit checked at charge tail
127	
128	  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
129	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBCC_rbrr_file}"
130	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRD="${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
131	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
132	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
133	
134	  # RBDC env file — RBDC_* are bash-kindle constants invisible to compose
135	  # without an env-file bridge. Write the subset compose interpolates into
136	  # a temp file and feed it via --env-file.
137	  local z_rbdc_env="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_rbdc_compose.env"
138	  printf 'RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=%s\nRBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY=%s\n' \
139	    "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}" > "${z_rbdc_env}" \
140	    || buc_die "Failed to write RBDC compose env file: ${z_rbdc_env}"
141	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC="${z_rbdc_env}"
142	
143	
144	  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
145	  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
146	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
147	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
148	
149	  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
150	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
151	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
152	
153	  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
154	  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
155	  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
156	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
157	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
158	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
159	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
160	  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE

202	######################################################################
203	# Compose Command Helper
204	
205	# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
206	# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
207	zrbob_compose() {
208	  zrbob_sentinel
209	
210	  # Under Cygwin the runtime is Windows-native docker; the compose CLI opens
211	  # -f / --env-file / --project-directory as Windows paths, so a /cygdrive
212	  # absolute is misread ("does not exist") exactly as docker build/cp were.
213	  # Normalize every path arg to drive-letter form first; off Cygwin each
214	  # capture is identity. Pinning --project-directory to the native form
215	  # additionally makes compose resolve the fragment's relative volume mounts to
216	  # Windows-native paths — what Docker Desktop's WSL2 backend needs to bind-mount.
217	  local z_native_projdir=""
218	  z_native_projdir=$(buc_native_path_capture "${RBCC_moorings_dir}") \
219	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize compose --project-directory: ${RBCC_moorings_dir}"
220	  local z_native_rbrr=""
221	  z_native_rbrr=$(buc_native_path_capture "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}") \
222	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize compose --env-file (RBRR): ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}"
223	  local z_native_rbrd=""
224	  z_native_rbrd=$(buc_native_path_capture "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRD}") \
225	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize compose --env-file (RBRD): ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRD}"
226	  local z_native_rbdc=""
227	  z_native_rbdc=$(buc_native_path_capture "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC}") \
228	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize compose --env-file (RBDC): ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBDC}"
229	  local z_native_rbje=""
230	  z_native_rbje=$(buc_native_path_capture "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}") \
231	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize compose --env-file (RBJE): ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}"
232	  local z_native_rbrn=""
233	  z_native_rbrn=$(buc_native_path_capture "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}") \
234	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize compose --env-file (RBRN): ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}"
235	  local z_native_base=""
236	  z_native_base=$(buc_native_path_capture "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}") \
237	    || buc_die "Cannot normalize compose -f base: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
238	
239	  local z_args=()
240	  z_args+=("compose")
241	  # Pin the project directory to the moorings (consumer-config) root. Compose
242	  # otherwise derives it from the first -f file's location — and the base
243	  # compose now lives with the kit (Tools/rbk), not in moorings. Nameplate
244	  # fragment (rbnnh_compose.yml) relative paths — env_file, volume mounts —
245	  # are authored relative to this root, so it must be set explicitly. CLI
246	  # --env-file paths resolve against CWD (repo root) and are unaffected.
247	  z_args+=("--project-directory" "${z_native_projdir}")
248	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${z_native_rbrr}")
249	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${z_native_rbrd}")
250	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${z_native_rbdc}")
251	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${z_native_rbje}")
252	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${z_native_rbrn}")
253	  z_args+=("-f" "${z_native_base}")
254	
255	  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists (existence checked on the POSIX
256	  # path; the normalized native form is what compose receives)
257	  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
258	    local z_native_fragment=""
259	    z_native_fragment=$(buc_native_path_capture "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}") \
260	      || buc_die "Cannot normalize compose -f fragment: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"
261	    z_args+=("-f" "${z_native_fragment}")
262	  fi
263	
264	  z_args+=("-p" "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}")
265	  z_args+=("$@")
266	
267	  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
268	  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
269	}

271	######################################################################
272	# Auto-Summon Helpers
273	
274	# Discriminate kludge hallmarks (local-only, no GAR presence) from conjure /
275	# bind / graft hallmarks. Used by charge preflight to redirect the diagnostic
276	# away from the summon path, which is guaranteed to fail for kludge stamps.
277	zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge() {
278	  case "${1}" in
279	    "${RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE}"*) return 0 ;;
280	    *) return 1 ;;
281	  esac
282	}
283	
284	# Pull all three arks (image, about, vouch) for a hallmark to local runtime.
285	# Used when charge detects a missing local artifact and needs to bootstrap.
286	# Mirrors rbfr_summon's all-three-arks semantics; called inline rather than
287	# as a tabtarget invocation because rbob is the sole caller and avoids the
288	# launcher-dispatch round-trip.
289	zrbob_summon_full_hallmark() {
290	  local z_hallmark="${1:-}"
291	
292	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_summon_full_hallmark: hallmark required"
293	
294	  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
295	  local z_image_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
296	  local z_about_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}:${z_hallmark}"
297	  local z_vouch_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"
298	
299	  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
300	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
301	
302	  local z_token
303	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"
304	
305	  buc_step "Auto-summoning hallmark ${z_hallmark} (image + about + vouch)"
306	
307	  rbgo_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_registry_host}"
308	
309	  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image ark: ${z_image_ref}"
310	  docker pull "${z_about_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull about ark: ${z_about_ref}"
311	  docker pull "${z_vouch_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull vouch ark: ${z_vouch_ref}"
312	
313	  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_hallmark}"
314	}
315	
316	# Pull a single image ref to local runtime — used by the image-only auto-summon
317	# branch in rbob_charge as defense-in-depth for partial-state cases (vouch
318	# present but image missing). The full-hallmark helper above covers the common
319	# path where all arks are missing together.
320	# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
321	zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
322	  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
323	  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
324	  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"
325	
326	  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
327	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
328	  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"
329	
330	  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
331	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
332	
333	  local z_token
334	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"
335	
336	  # Pull the image
337	  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"
338	
339	  rbgo_docker_login "${z_token}" "${z_registry_host}"
340	
341	  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"
342	
343	  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
344	}

346	######################################################################
347	# Charge Invariant — Subnet-Keyed Reclaim
348	
349	# At most one crucible per nameplate per system. A crucible's subnet derives
350	# from the nameplate (RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP), not from the project prefix, so
351	# two same-nameplate crucibles can never coexist on one daemon. Free the
352	# nameplate's subnet by tearing down whatever network occupies it — regardless
353	# of prefix or how the network was created (compose or manual `docker network
354	# create`). Match on the subnet, a structural fact, not on inferred name
355	# lineage. Our own compose network is excluded; compose-down already handled it.
356	zrbob_reclaim_subnet() {
357	  zrbob_sentinel
358	
359	  local -r z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
360	  local -r z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_networks.txt"
361	  local -r z_networks_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_networks_stderr.txt"
362	  local -r z_inspect_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_inspect_"
363	  local -r z_containers_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_containers_"
364	  local -r z_crm_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_crm_"
365	  local -r z_nrm_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_reclaim_nrm_"
366	
367	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' \
368	    > "${z_networks_file}" 2>"${z_networks_stderr}" \
369	    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks — see ${z_networks_stderr}"
370	
371	  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG; names are captured before any
372	  # reclaim, so removing networks mid-loop cannot disturb the iteration)
373	  local z_names=()
374	  local z_line=""
375	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
376	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
377	    z_names+=("${z_line}")
378	  done < "${z_networks_file}"
379	
380	  local z_index=0
381	  local z_name=""
382	  local z_subnet_file=""
383	  local z_subnet_stderr=""
384	  local z_subnet=""
385	  local z_containers_file=""
386	  local z_containers_stderr=""
387	  local z_crm_file=""
388	  local z_nrm_file=""
389	
390	  if (( ${#z_names[@]} )); then
391	    for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
392	      z_subnet_file="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
393	      z_subnet_stderr="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}_stderr.txt"
394	      z_containers_file="${z_containers_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
395	      z_containers_stderr="${z_containers_prefix}${z_index}_stderr.txt"
396	      z_crm_file="${z_crm_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
397	      z_nrm_file="${z_nrm_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
398	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
399	
400	      # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
401	      test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue
402	
403	      # A network in the list may vanish or lack IPAM config between ls and
404	      # inspect; tolerate by skipping (stderr preserved for forensics)
405	      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
406	        --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
407	        > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>"${z_subnet_stderr}" \
408	        || continue
409	
410	      z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")
411	      test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}" || continue
412	
413	      buc_warn "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} occupied by stale network '${z_name}' — reclaiming"
414	
415	      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ps -aq --filter "network=${z_name}" \
416	        > "${z_containers_file}" 2>"${z_containers_stderr}" \
417	        || buc_die "Failed to list containers on '${z_name}' — see ${z_containers_stderr}"
418	
419	      local z_containers=()
420	      local z_cline=""
421	      while IFS= read -r z_cline || test -n "${z_cline}"; do
422	        test -n "${z_cline}" || continue
423	        z_containers+=("${z_cline}")
424	      done < "${z_containers_file}"
425	
426	      # Force-remove attached containers first (a mid-run-killed crucible may
427	      # leave them running). Non-fatal: a container may already be exiting; the
428	      # network rm below is the real gate — if removal truly failed there, that
429	      # rm fails and dies.
430	      if (( ${#z_containers[@]} )); then
431	        ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} rm -f "${z_containers[@]}" \
432	          > "${z_crm_file}" 2>&1 \
433	          || buc_warn "Some containers on '${z_name}' resisted removal — see ${z_crm_file}"
434	      fi
435	
436	      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network rm "${z_name}" \
437	        > "${z_nrm_file}" 2>&1 \
438	        || buc_die "Failed to reclaim subnet ${z_target_subnet}: could not remove network '${z_name}' — see ${z_nrm_file}"
439	
440	      buc_info "Reclaimed subnet ${z_target_subnet} from '${z_name}'"
441	    done
442	  fi
443	}

445	######################################################################
446	# Public API
447	
448	# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
449	# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
450	rbob_charge() {
451	  zrbob_sentinel
452	
453	  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
454	
455	  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
456	  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
457	    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
458	  fi
459	
460	  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
461	  rbrn_preflight
462	
463	  # Preflight: ensure all hallmark arks exist locally. Vouch presence is the
464	  # hallmark-level signal — if vouch is missing, the full hallmark is treated
465	  # as not-yet-summoned and all three arks are pulled together. Image-only
466	  # auto-summon below is defense-in-depth for partial-state cases.
467	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
468	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
469	      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
470	    fi
471	    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
472	    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
473	  fi
474	
475	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
476	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
477	      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
478	    fi
479	    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
480	    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
481	  fi
482	
483	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
484	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
485	      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
486	    fi
487	    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
488	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
489	  fi
490	
491	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
492	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
493	      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
494	    fi
495	    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
496	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
497	  fi
498	
499	  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
500	  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state (quenching any preexisting can take a couple of minutes)"
501	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
502	
503	  # Charge invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system. A
504	  # crucible's subnet derives from the nameplate, not the prefix, so freeing
505	  # the nameplate's subnet — tearing down whatever network occupies it,
506	  # regardless of prefix or creation method — is sufficient and subsumes any
507	  # name-lineage collision check.
508	  zrbob_reclaim_subnet
509	
510	  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
511	  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
512	  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
513	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait
514	
515	  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
516	
517	  if test "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}" -gt 0; then
518	    buc_step "Waiting ${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}s for bottle service readiness"
519	    sleep "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}"
520	  fi
521	
522	  if test -x "${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"; then
523	    buc_step "Running post-charge hook: ${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"
524	    "${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}" || buc_die "Post-charge hook failed: ${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"
525	  fi
526	}
527	
528	# Check whether the crucible is charged — sentry, pentacle, and bottle must each
529	# be individually `running`. BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not.
530	# Never dies, no output. Compose stderr captured to BURD_TEMP_DIR for operator
531	# inspection on verify-active failures.
532	rbob_charged_predicate() {
533	  zrbob_sentinel
534	
535	  local z_service=""
536	  local z_ids_file=""
537	  local z_stderr_file=""
538	
539	  for z_service in "${RBCC_container_sentry}" "${RBCC_container_pentacle}" "${RBCC_container_bottle}"; do
540	    z_ids_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_charged_${z_service}_ids.txt"
541	    z_stderr_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_charged_${z_service}_stderr.txt"
542	
543	    "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" compose -p "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}" ps "${z_service}" --status running -q \
544	      > "${z_ids_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
545	      || return 1
546	
547	    test -s "${z_ids_file}" || return 1
548	  done
549	
550	  return 0
551	}

602	# Bark bottle — non-interactive command execution in bottle

NOTE: Bottle container creation is managed by Docker Compose.
The bottle service definition specifies the bottle image, `network_mode: "service:pentacle"` (shares pentacle's network namespace), `cap_drop: NET_RAW, NET_ADMIN` and `security_opt: label:disable` (kernel-layer capability bounds), and a `sessile` profile gate.

.. `security_opt: label:disable` disables SELinux/AppArmor labelling for the bottle, required for the volume bind-mount workflow used by per-nameplate compose fragments.
The bottle starts with a pre-configured network path through the sentry.
Security enforcement is active from the first packet.

input.command:
cat << 'EOF'
RBJS_SENTRY.SH - CONCEPTUAL COMMENTS (security/design rationale):

1. rbjs_sentry.sh:53-57
   "Docker assigns the container's default route to whichever attached network's gateway it picks (alphabetically-first attached, empirically); compose 'priority' is not honored. Same don't-trust-Docker discipline as interface naming above — sentry takes ownership: derive uplink gateway as first usable IP of uplink subnet (Docker bridge convention) and install default route via uplink."

2. rbjs_sentry.sh:122-130
   "rp_filter=1 (strict) requires the packet's source-IP reverse-path to match the ingress interface. Inbound published-port traffic from the framework gateway (e.g., Docker Desktop 192.168.65.1) arrives on whichever interface Docker chose for port-publish delivery, but its reverse route is always via the default gateway on uplink — strict mode mismatches and drops at routing time, before the iptables FORWARD chain ever sees the packet. Loose mode (rp_filter=2) allows any source IP that's reachable via any interface, which is the actual invariant for entry-port traffic. Iptables rules below still gate authorization; rp_filter is not the load-bearing security control."

3. rbjs_sentry.sh:134-144
   "Classification: destination port + per-IP source exclusion of the two enclave-internal containers (sentry and bottle/pentacle). RETURN short-circuit pattern — if source matches either enclave container IP, skip the DNAT; otherwise the unconditional DNAT below fires. Why per-IP and not whole-CIDR (! -s ENCLAVE_CIDR): on linux Docker Engine the host attaches to the enclave bridge as a peer with the bridge gateway IP (.1), which is inside the enclave CIDR by Docker convention. Whole-CIDR exclusion rejects every legitimate host SYN (empirically confirmed on cerebro: PREROUTING DNAT fired 0 times). Per-IP excludes exactly the threat surface — the two enclave container IPs known to compose — and lets the bridge gateway, Desktop's VM gateway (192.168.65.1), rootlesskit's synthetic source (10.0.2.100), and external clients all pass."

4. rbjs_sentry.sh:155-160
   "Return-path symmetry: MASQUERADE rewrites the source of the post-DNAT packet to sentry's enclave IP, so the bottle's reply destination is in-enclave. The bottle then routes via its directly-connected enclave route back to sentry, where conntrack reverses the NAT cleanly. Without this, the bottle would reply to the original (possibly framework-rewritten) source IP, traversing default-via-sentry into transit — empirically broken on Docker Desktop per the topology-reframe scry diagnostic."

5. rbjs_sentry.sh:164-167
   "Authorization: only flows that sentry's own PREROUTING DNAT created conntrack state for are allowed to forward. Conntrack DNAT-state is unforgeable from either bridge. The parent FORWARD chain's ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT (line above) handles the return path before reaching RBM-FORWARD."

EOF

   "Under Cygwin the runtime is Windows-native docker; the compose CLI opens -f / --env-file / --project-directory as Windows paths, so a /cygdrive absolute is misread ('does not exist') exactly as docker build/cp were. Normalize every path arg to drive-letter form first; off Cygwin each capture is identity. Pinning --project-directory to the native form additionally makes compose resolve the fragment's relative volume mounts to Windows-native paths — what Docker Desktop's WSL2 backend needs to bind-mount."

8. rbob_bottle.sh:349-355
   "At most one crucible per nameplate per system. A crucible's subnet derives from the nameplate (RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP), not from the project prefix, so two same-nameplate crucibles can never coexist on one daemon. Free the nameplate's subnet by tearing down whatever network occupies it — regardless of prefix or how the network was created (compose or manual `docker network create`). Match on the subnet, a structural fact, not on inferred name lineage. Our own compose network is excluded; compose-down already handled it."

EOF
